Himachal Pradesh likely to witness dry spell after May 18, temperatures may touch 40°C: IMD
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast light rainfall activity in isolated higher reaches of Himachal Pradesh over the next two days.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast light rainfall activity in isolated higher reaches of Himachal Pradesh over the next two days, while warning of a prolonged dry spell and rising temperatures across the state after May 18.
Speaking to ANI, Head IMD Shobhit Katiyar said no major weather activity is expected in most parts of the state during the next three days, although isolated light rain spells may occur in higher hill districts, including Chamba, Lahaul-Spiti and Kullu.
"From May 18 to May 23, the weather across Himachal Pradesh is expected to remain dry. However, due to a fresh western disturbance, rainfall activity may resume in the state after May 24," Katiyar said.

He said daytime temperatures are currently ranging between 30-35 degrees Celsius in lower hill and plain areas, 24 to 30 degrees Celsius in the mid-hill regions, and 18 to 24 degrees Celsius in higher reaches.
Katiyar further said temperatures are likely to rise steadily over the next week due to the absence of significant weather systems.

title: Himachal Pradesh to enter dry spell after May 18 with temperatures nearing 40°C, IMD forecasts - The News Mill
search_term: heatwave (Bilaspur OR Chamba OR Hamirpur OR Kangra OR Kinnaur OR Kullu OR "Lahaul and Spiti" OR Mandi OR Shimla OR "Sirmaur (Sirmour)" OR Solan OR Una) Himachal Pradesh
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecast light rainfall in isolated higher regions of Himachal Pradesh over the next two days, with a prolonged dry spell and rising temperatures expected across the state after May 18.
Shobhit Katiyar, Head of IMD, said no significant weather activity is anticipated in most parts of the state during the coming three days, although isolated light rain may occur in hill districts such as Chamba, Lahaul-Spiti, and Kullu.
He stated, “From May 18 to May 23, the weather across Himachal Pradesh is expected to remain dry. However, due to a fresh western disturbance, rainfall activity may resume in the state after May 24.”
Daytime temperatures currently range between 30 and 35 degrees Celsius in lower hill and plain areas, 24 to 30 degrees Celsius in mid-hill regions, and 18 to 24 degrees Celsius in the higher reaches.
Katiyar added that temperatures are likely to rise steadily over the next week owing to the absence of significant weather systems. “In the next 3-4 days, temperatures in lower hill and plain areas may touch around 40 degrees Celsius,” he said. Una district recorded a maximum temperature of 36.2 degrees Celsius on Thursday, while neighbouring areas saw temperatures between 34 and 35 degrees Celsius.
The IMD official noted the state has received above-normal rainfall during May. Against the normal rainfall of approximately 33 mm from May 1 to May 14, Himachal Pradesh recorded 37 mm, about 13 per cent above normal.
However, cumulative pre-monsoon rainfall from March 1 to May 14 stood at 197 mm compared to the normal 210 mm, reflecting a deficit of nearly 6 per cent.
Regarding heatwave conditions, Katiyar said no heatwave alert has been issued so far but warned that heatwave-like conditions may develop in isolated areas around May 20 to 22. “We may issue a heatwave alert in the next one to two days, depending on evolving conditions,” he added.
